seo.title: Building AI Chatbot with AG2 + Vex Guardrails
seo.description: Learn how to build ai chatbot with AG2 + Vex guardrails. Detect failures, prevent drift, and ensure reliability.

content.intro: Building AI Chatbots with AG2 leverages its multi-agent conversation capabilities for dynamic, retrieval-augmented interactions with intent classification and memory management. Guardrails are essential to prevent conversation drift, factual inaccuracies, and safety violations in high-risk deployments. Vex provides runtime verification to ensure response accuracy, coherence, and PII protection.

architectureOverview.heading: AI Chatbot Architecture with AG2
architectureOverview.description:
The AG2 AI Chatbot architecture features a central ConversationalAgent handling user inputs through intent classification, routing to specialized sub-agents like RetrievalAgent for RAG and MemoryAgent for context persistence. Multi-turn conversations maintain state via shared memory while group chat dynamics enable collaborative reasoning across agents. Safety and verification layers intercept messages to enforce coherence and prevent harmful outputs before final response generation.

Intent classification triggers appropriate agent workflows: factual queries activate retrieval paths, while complex reasoning engages multi-agent deliberation. Vex integrates as a message hook across all agents, verifying outputs against accuracy, safety, and coherence policies in real-time.

This setup scales to handle diverse conversational needs while maintaining strict reliability through continuous monitoring and correction.

risks.name: Agent conversation spiraling off-topic
risks.description: Multi-turn conversations drift from user intent due to context accumulation or agent misalignment
risks.mitigation: Vex coherence checks enforce topical relevance across conversation turns

risks.name: Hallucination in RAG responses
risks.description: RetrievalAgent generates unsupported facts when retrieved context is insufficient
risks.mitigation: Vex accuracy verification cross-checks responses against retrieved documents

risks.name: PII leakage in memory storage
risks.description: MemoryAgent persists sensitive user data without redaction
risks.mitigation: Vex PII protection scans and masks identifiers before memory storage

risks.name: Safety violations in generated responses
risks.description: ConversationalAgent produces harmful or inappropriate content
risks.mitigation: Vex safety filtering blocks unsafe outputs before user delivery

implementation.title: Setup AG2 Conversational Agent
implementation.description: Initialize core chatbot with intent classification and Vex hook registration
implementation.code:
from ag2 import ConversationalAgent, GroupChat
from vex import VexClient

vex = VexClient(api_key="your-vex-key")

chatbot = ConversationalAgent(
    name="Chatbot",
    llm_config={"model": "gpt-4o"},
    message_hook=vex.verify_message,
    max_turns=10
)

group_chat = GroupChat(agents=[chatbot], message_hook=vex.group_verify)

implementation.title: RAG Retrieval Agent Integration
implementation.description: Add retrieval capabilities with hallucination detection
implementation.code:
from ag2 import RetrievalAgent

retriever = RetrievalAgent(
    name="Retriever",
    vector_store="your-vector-db",
    llm_config={"model": "gpt-4o"},
    message_hook=lambda msg: vex.verify(
        msg, 
        checks=["accuracy", "hallucination"]
    ),
    top_k=5
)

chatbot.register_subagent(retriever)

implementation.title: Memory Management with PII Protection
implementation.description: Implement conversation memory with automatic PII redaction
implementation.code:
from ag2 import MemoryAgent

memory = MemoryAgent(
    name="Memory",
    max_history=20,
    message_hook=lambda msg: vex.verify(
        msg,
        checks=["pii_protection", "coherence"],
        redact_pii=True
    )
)

group_chat.add_memory(memory)
chatbot.set_memory(memory)

implementation.title: Multi-turn Chat Handler
implementation.description: Orchestrate complete conversation flow with verification
implementation.code:
def handle_conversation(user_input: str, context: dict = None):
    verified_input = vex.verify(user_input, checks=["safety", "intent"])
    
    response = group_chat.initiate_chat(
        messages=[{"role": "user", "content": verified_input}],
        max_rounds=5
    )
    
    final_response = vex.verify(
        response[-1]["content"],
        checks=["accuracy", "safety", "coherence"]
    )
    
    return final_response

# Usage
result = handle_conversation("What's the capital of France?")

vexIntegration.title: Register Vex Message Hook
vexIntegration.description: Global message verification across all AG2 agents

vexIntegration.title: Custom Group Chat Verification
vexIntegration.description: Consensus verification for multi-agent group chats

vexIntegration.title: Real-time Conversation Monitoring
vexIntegration.description: Dashboard monitoring and intervention for live chats
vexIntegration.code:
from vex import Monitor
from ag2 import ConversationalAgent

monitor = Monitor(
    project="ag2-chatbot",
    alerts={
        "drift_detected": "coherence < 0.7",
        "pii_leak": "pii_detected > 0",
        "hallucination": "accuracy < 0.8"
    }
)

chatbot.message_hook = monitor.track(vex.verify_message)

# Auto-intervention example
def intervention_hook(message):
    verdict = vex.verify(message)
    if verdict["coherence"] < 0.7:
        return "Let's refocus on your original question."
    return verdict["content"]

chatbot.set_intervention_hook(intervention_hook)

cta.heading: Secure Your AG2 AI Chatbot
cta.description: Install Vex today with `pip install ag2 vex-sdk` and register your message hooks to protect against conversation failures.
